content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 14, 1994

Leonard Tyler
12380 Fourth Street, Space 21
Yucaipa, CA 92399

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-94-071

Dear Mr. Tyler:

This is in response to your letter requesting information
regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1

Please note, the Commission provides advice to persons whose
duties under the Act are in question. (Section 83114(b).) In
addition, requests for formal written advice will only be acted
upon if all the facts with respect to a specific decision have
been provided. (Regulation 18329, copy enclosed.)

Since you are not a currently a city councilmember, and your
request is general in nature, we are treating your request as one
for informal assistance.2 We have enclosed a variety of general
materials regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions which
should answer the questions you pose.

Should you have further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. Commission regulations
appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000-18954.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked general questions about conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, though the specific details of the inquiry are not provided in this letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that because the requestor is not currently a city councilmember and the request was general in nature, it would be treated as an informal assistance request rather than a formal written advice request. The FPPC provided general materials on conflict-of-interest provisions and offered further assistance if needed.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a brief informal assistance letter with no formal question or conclusion sections. The letter primarily explains the FPPC's classification of the request and directs the requestor to enclosed materials. Specific questions posed by the requestor are not detailed in this response letter. The document includes a footnote clarifying that informal assistance does not provide immunity like formal written advice.

QUESTION: The requestor asked general questions about conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, though the specific details of the inquiry are not provided in this letter.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC determined that because the requestor is not currently a city councilmember and the request was general in nature, it would be treated as an informal assistance request rather than a formal written advice request. The FPPC provided general materials on conflict-of-interest provisions and offered further assistance if needed.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to Leonard Tyler's general request for information about conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act by classifying it as an informal assistance request and providing general educational materials rather than formal written advice.
